objective: Make the Society of Minds waterfall and external-services runbook an enforced session contract so Codex starts in supervisor mode, uses the approved Claude/Codex CLI path, and requires plan, alternate-family review, handoff, worker execution, QA, and deterministic gate sequencing before implementation-ready work proceeds.
scope_boundary: Define and enforce a tracked session contract for Codex sessions.
Require external-services runbook loading and exact governed CLI/auth/bootstrap path visibility at session start.
Tighten plan, handoff, and consumer-package validation only as needed to require the waterfall lifecycle for implementation-ready governance work.
Preserve downstream repo adoption as issue-backed follow-up work after the governance source changes land.
out_of_scope: Direct downstream repo mutations in Stampede or other governed product repos.
A new autonomous orchestration layer or daemon.
Weakening planner-boundary, lane-claim, or alternate-review rules.
Replacing the external-services runbook with duplicated inline instructions.
research_summary: Read-only audits confirmed the systemic gap. The governance repo documents the waterfall in STANDARDS.md and the CLI/auth/bootstrap path in docs/EXTERNAL_SERVICES_RUNBOOK.md, but session-start wiring only injects wiki/index.md and graph/report context. There is no tracked CODEX.md session contract, no bootstrap sentinel proving the runbook was loaded, and no consumer/verifier or plan/handoff enforcement that makes supervisor-first plus alternate-family review unavoidable for implementation-ready waterfall work.

sprints.name: Session contract source of truth
sprints.goal: Create a tracked Codex-facing session contract and bootstrap checklist that forces the supervisor-first waterfall and the external-services runbook path into session start.
tasks: Add a tracked CODEX.md contract for governance sessions.
Expand the session-start contract in CLAUDE.md, STANDARDS.md, and docs/EXTERNAL_SERVICES_RUNBOOK.md.
Refactor the governance pre-session hook path so the bootstrap checklist is canonical and machine-checkable.
Ensure the bootstrap output surfaces exact governed CLI/auth/bootstrap paths instead of generic discovery.
Bring .claude/settings.json up to the STANDARDS-required PostToolUse matcher contract for check-errors coverage.
Track the compatible .claude wrapper path in git so CI can verify the local hook shim that settings.json invokes.
acceptance_criteria: A tracked CODEX.md explicitly states Codex is the supervisor/orchestrator, delegates bounded work, and does not collapse planning, worker, review, and QA authority into one role by default.

Session start requires or emits a deterministic checklist covering CODEX.md, CLAUDE.md, docs/PROGRESS.md, docs/FAIL_FAST_LOG.md, STANDARDS.md Society of Minds, docs/EXTERNAL_SERVICES_RUNBOOK.md, and pending Codex backlog status.
The bootstrap output cites the approved Claude/Codex CLI and auth/bootstrap path from the external-services runbook rather than leaving discovery to ad hoc search.
The governance repo uses one canonical pre-session implementation path instead of allowing duplicate hook behavior to drift.

The session-start path emits a deterministic, machine-checkable sentinel proving CODEX.md, docs/EXTERNAL_SERVICES_RUNBOOK.md, and STANDARDS.md Society of Minds were loaded or surfaced, with warnings when a required contract file is missing.
.claude/settings.json wires the STANDARDS-required PostToolUse matcher '*' for check-errors coverage, or a documented exception is recorded.

sprints.name: Waterfall evidence enforcement
sprints.goal: Tighten validators and package surfaces so implementation-ready governance work requires alternate-family review, handoff evidence, QA evidence, and required session-contract surfaces.
tasks: Tighten structured-plan validation for implementation-ready waterfall work.
Tighten handoff validation for review and QA evidence expectations.
Extend governance package and consumer verification surfaces to represent the tracked session contract and managed hook expectations.
Wire the stricter checks into governance CI and the governance local-CI profile.
Add a reusable session-contract surface validator so GitHub Actions and local CI enforce the same bootstrap wiring.
acceptance_criteria: Implementation-ready governance waterfall plans require alternate_model_review.required to be true and accepted before code changes are considered governance-ready.
Implementation-ready governance waterfall plans require non-empty handoff package, validation artifact, review artifact, and QA gate references.
The package/verifier contract can represent tracked session-contract files and managed hook expectations without weakening stricter repo-local policies.
Governance CI and local CI both fail when required session-contract or waterfall-evidence surfaces are missing.
validate_handoff_package.py rejects implementation_ready, in_progress, validation_ready, and accepted handoffs when review_artifact_refs or gate_artifact_refs are empty.

validate_structured_agent_cycle_plan.py rejects implementation-ready plans with required alternate_model_review statuses outside accepted states in all validation paths for issue-573-and-later implementation-ready plans, without retroactively invalidating preserved historical artifacts.
A reusable session-contract surface validator proves both UserPromptSubmit bootstrap wiring and PostToolUse '*' check-errors coverage in GitHub Actions and local CI.

sprints.name: Issue evidence and mirrors
sprints.goal: Keep the issue-backed governance evidence chain and repo mirrors aligned with the new session contract.
tasks: Update docs/PROGRESS.md, docs/FEATURE_REGISTRY.md, and docs/FAIL_FAST_LOG.md for the new contract and drift pattern.
Capture alternate-family review, validation, and closeout evidence.
Preserve any residual downstream adoption work as follow-up issues instead of broadening this branch.
acceptance_criteria: PROGRESS, FEATURE_REGISTRY, and FAIL_FAST_LOG record the session-contract change and the failure mode it prevents.
The issue evidence chain includes PDCA/R, execution scope, handoff package, alternate-family review, validation artifact, and closeout.
Any downstream adoption work remains issue-backed follow-up work.

specialist_reviews.focus: Current bootstrap behavior versus the desired supervisor-first waterfall and external-services runbook loading path.
specialist_reviews.summary: Confirmed that governance session start currently injects only wiki/graph context, does not require runbook loading, has no bootstrap sentinel, and has no tracked CODEX.md contract.

specialist_reviews.focus: Current plan, handoff, QA, and consumer-package enforcement gaps that still allow orchestrator or review drift.
specialist_reviews.summary: Confirmed that the repo already enforces many artifact shapes, but implementation-ready work can still avoid mandatory alternate-family review, strong handoff refs, and session-contract package coverage.

alternate_model_review.summary: Claude Opus 4.6 approved the planning packet with changes: add a machine-checkable bootstrap sentinel, require non-empty review/gate refs in implementation handoffs, make implementation-ready alternate-review acceptance unconditional across validation paths, and bring .claude/settings.json to the STANDARDS-required PostToolUse '*' contract.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Implement the governance-first session contract, bootstrap sentinel, validator/package enforcement, CI/local-CI wiring, and issue evidence for issue #573 only.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Implementation completed. Final state is deterministic validation, Stage 6 closeout evidence, and QA review before PR publication.
handoff_acceptance_criteria: The planning packet is reviewed through the governed Claude CLI path defined by docs/EXTERNAL_SERVICES_RUNBOOK.md.
The implementation slice must add a machine-checkable bootstrap sentinel and the required validator/package enforcement identified in the alternate-family review.
The final implementation branch retains supervisor-first, alternate-review, handoff, and QA evidence requirements.

material_deviation_rules: If downstream repo adoption is required, stop and open issue-backed follow-up work instead of editing consumer repos in this branch.
If the current hook shape cannot enforce the contract without duplication, introduce one governed wrapper/helper rather than diverging multiple hook copies.
If alternate-family review is unavailable, log the governed availability failure and stop instead of implementing governance-surface changes without the review.
If same-family Codex planning and Codex subagent implementation extends beyond the active exception window, renew the issue-backed exception or switch the implementation path before continuing.
approved_by: operator approval: hand plan to Opus for review, then update PDCA/R, detailed ACs, implement, verify
